senses.definition: to tell someone to do something by ordering or instructing them

examples.x: The judge enjoined the defendant from contacting the plaintiff while the case was pending.
The teacher enjoined the students to complete their assignments by the end of the week.
The doctor enjoined the patient to follow a strict diet and exercise regimen to improve their health.
The law enjoins drivers to obey all traffic signs and signals for the safety of themselves and others.

senses.definition: information, criticism, or advice about a person's performance, a new product, etc. intended for improvement

examples.x: The teacher gave constructive feedback to help improve his writing skills.
Customer feedback is essential for improving product quality.
I appreciate your feedback on my proposal; it will help me refine it.
Feedback from the audience can help shape the performance.

senses.definition: to act accordingly to someone or something's advice, commands, or instructions

examples.x: She followed her doctor's advice and improved her health.
He has trouble following complex directions.
The soldiers claimed they were just following orders during the mission.
She followed the recipe loosely, adding her own twist to the dish.
Follow the arrows on the floor to navigate through the museum.

senses.definition: help and advice about how to solve a problem, given by someone who is knowledgeable and experienced

examples.x: The mentor provided invaluable guidance to the new employee, helping them navigate the intricacies of the job.
The financial advisor gave sound guidance on investment options, helping clients make informed decisions about their money.
The consultant offered guidance to small business owners, helping them develop effective strategies for growth and success.
The spiritual leader offered guidance to members of the community, providing wisdom and support during times of crisis.
The career counselor offered guidance to job seekers, assisting them with resume writing, interview skills, and job search strategies.

senses.definition: to direct or influence someone's motivation or behavior

examples.x: The teacher's advice helped to guide her students toward success.
Parents often try to guide their children in making good decisions.
His mentor worked hard to guide him through his career challenges.
She used her experience to guide the team in the right direction.
The coach's encouragement was crucial to guide the players' motivation.

senses.definition: offering helpful advice
to provide abstract or intangible things, such as punishments, compliments, judgments, advice, etc., to someone

examples.x: The judge handed out a harsh punishment to the defendant for their involvement in the crime.
The counselor handed out valuable advice to the troubled teenagers, guiding them toward making better life choices.
The critic was known for handing out scathing judgments on the quality of films, which sometimes led to controversy in the industry.
The teacher handed out compliments to the student for their outstanding performance on the project.

senses.definition: used to indicate an obligation or to emphasize the necessity of something happening

examples.x: I have to finish this report by the end of the day.
We have to pay our bills before the due date to avoid late fees.
We have to be at the airport two hours before the flight.
They have to study hard for their exams next week.
He has to pick up his kids from school at 3 PM.

senses.definition: a warning or notification provided in advance to inform someone about a situation, often to prepare them for what is coming
to be attentive to advice or a warning

examples.x: It's important to heed the advice of experienced hikers when trekking in unfamiliar terrain.
She failed to heed the warning signs and ended up getting lost in the forest.
The coach urged his players to heed his instructions if they wanted to win the game.
Drivers should always heed traffic signals to avoid accidents on the road.
Despite her friends' warnings, she chose not to heed them and continued with her risky behavior.

senses.definition: a person or thing that provides assistance, making it easier or possible to accomplish something

examples.x: The new software was a great help in organizing the company’s data.
She was a valuable help during the move, carrying boxes and arranging furniture.
His experience was a big help in solving the problem quickly.
The manual was a useful help when assembling the furniture.
He sought help from a tutor to improve his understanding of the subject.

senses.definition: a phone service that provides advice, comfort, or information regarding specific problems

examples.x: She contacted the helpline to get advice on how to handle the emergency.
After losing my job, I called the helpline to learn about available resources for unemployment benefits.
I called the helpline for assistance with my Internet connection issues.

senses.definition: a speech or a piece of writing that is meant to advise people on the correct way of behaving

examples.x: In his homily, the Rev. Thomas spoke about practicing compassion even for those who wrong us.
She found the weekly homilies filled with wisdom and insight into applying faith to daily life.

senses.definition: giving thorough instructions on a particular matter

examples.x: She followed a how-to guide to assemble the furniture, which provided thorough instructions and illustrations.
The how-to video on YouTube offered step-by-step guidance for installing a new kitchen sink.
He relied on a how-to manual to learn the basics of car maintenance, including changing the oil and checking tire pressure.
The how-to article in the magazine detailed the process of starting a small business, from writing a business plan to securing funding.
The online course provided a comprehensive how-to curriculum for learning digital marketing strategies.

senses.definition: ‌used to offer advice or instructions to someone who is incapable of making decisions

examples.x: When faced with a tough decision, if in doubt, seek advice from a mentor.
Double-check your figures; if in doubt, verify your calculations.
Review the guidelines carefully; if in doubt, follow the established procedures.
If in doubt, don't hesitate to ask for help to ensure you’re on the right track.
Before finalizing your report, if in doubt, ask a colleague to review it.

senses.definition: used to say what choices or actions one would make if one was in another person's situation

examples.x: If I were in her place, I would have taken the job offer without hesitation.
If I were in your place, I'd definitely consider taking a vacation to relax.
I don't know how he copes with so much pressure; I couldn't handle it in his place.
In your place, I'd consider seeking legal advice to address the issue.
If I were in their place, I'd focus on building a strong online presence for the business.

senses.definition: used to tell someone what is better for them to do

examples.x: If I were you, I would double-check the directions before heading out to avoid getting lost.
If I were you, I'd take some time off to recharge and relax after such a hectic week.
If I were you, I wouldn't hesitate to ask for a raise considering all the hard work you've put in.
If I were you, I'd get a second opinion from another doctor before making a decision about surgery.
If I were you, I'd start saving for retirement as early as possible to secure financial stability in the future.

senses.definition: (medical) to advise and authorize a treatment or procedure due to a particular condition or circumstance

examples.x: The doctor indicated surgery to remove the tumor due to its size and location.
The dentist indicated a root canal procedure to address the infection in the patient's tooth.
The oncologist indicated chemotherapy as the appropriate treatment for the patient's cancer.
Due to the severity of the infection, the veterinarian indicated antibiotics for the sick dog.
The psychiatrist indicated cognitive-behavioral therapy for the patient's anxiety disorder.

senses.definition: not recommended to do based on the particular situation

examples.x: Accepting that job offer would be inadvisable since you've only just started your current role.
It would be inadvisable to confront your boss publicly before gathering more information.
Taking an extended vacation so close to deadline would be inadvisable given current workloads.
Eating raw chicken is highly inadvisable due to the risk of food poisoning.
It's inadvisable to ignore the doctor's orders regarding medication.

senses.definition: a type of psychotherapy for married couples that helps them understand and resolve conflicts in order to improve their relationship
someone who is advised or trained under the supervision of a mentor
a reliable and experienced person who helps those with less experience

examples.x: The aspiring artist found a mentor who provided valuable feedback and encouragement to help him develop his artistic skills.
Mentors play an important role in welcoming new employees and helping them adapt to workplace culture.
As a young entrepreneur, she sought guidance from an experienced mentor who helped her navigate the challenges of starting a business.
The mentor provided invaluable advice and support to the new employees, helping them acclimate to their roles within the company.
The mentor encouraged her mentee to set ambitious goals and provided the necessary resources and encouragement to help them achieve success.

senses.definition: the practice of offering advice or helping a younger or less experienced individual over a period of time regarding their job or a particular subject
the guidance, help, or advice given by a mentor, particularly in a company or educational institution
